name: Raspberry Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly crush 2 cups of the berries with a fork and place in a quart-size glass canning jar or any other jar with an enamel-lined lid.
2. Pour enough brandy into the jar to cover the berries.
3. Add equal portions of the remaining berries and brandy, ensuring all berries are covered by brandy.
4. Pour just enough brandy to begin a small overflow, then tightly cap the jar and wipe the outside clean.
5. Allow to steep in a cool, dark place for 2 months. Alternatively, tape black construction paper around the jar.
6. Every 2 weeks, gently shake the jar to distribute flavors.
7. After 2 months, pour contents through a regular strainer or sieve; discard residue.
8. Strain again through slightly dampened cheesecloth or a large clean coffee filter placed inside a funnel; loosely cover with plastic wrap (process may take several hours).
9. In a small pan, combine sugar, water, and lemon zest. Bring to boil over moderate-high heat, then simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until cooled to room temperature.
10. Add glycerine if desired.
11. Pour the sugar syrup through a strainer into the jar to remove zest.
12. Tighten lid and shake to blend. Let mature at room temperature or slightly cooler for at least 1 month. Vodka can replace brandy.
